title: 1. Starting a Retreat
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and pedagogically structured treatment of Taking Refuge, explaining the ceremony in full context with the three refuges (Buddha, Dhamma, Sangha), the precepts, and their spiritual significance. It combines practical instruction with deeper meaning, includes the actual refuge-taking ceremony with explanations, and offers rich teaching on what each refuge represents both historically and spiritually. The talk has excellent literary flow, moving from ceremony to meditation practice, making it an ideal spine for a chapter.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma opens a retreat by explaining the three refuges and precepts ceremony in detail, clarifying what it means to take refuge in Buddha (both historical and inner), Dhamma (teachings), and Sangha (community). He then guides practitioners through the ceremony itself while weaving in the deeper spiritual significance of commitment, faith, and the path to liberation.

relevance.note: Opens with traditional three refuges ceremony and explanation
